title: Understand: motion
description: Check your understanding of motion this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about speed and velocity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Speed and velocity mean exactly the same thing.
Speed is how fast an object moves and velocity is how far it moves.
Speed doesn't include direction and velocity does include direction.
When an object is moving straight it has speed, not velocity. When it's turning it has velocity, not speed.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements are true about speed and velocity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Speed is how fast an object moves and velocity is how far it moves.
When an object is moving straight it has speed, not velocity. When it's turning it has velocity, not speed.
Speed doesn't include direction and velocity does include direction.
Speed and velocity both tell you how fast an object is moving.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about speed and velocity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Speed and velocity mean exactly the same thing.
When an object is moving straight it has speed, not velocity. When it's turning it has velocity, not speed.
Speed includes direction, but velocity doesn't.
Speed and velocity both tell you how fast an object is moving.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph below models the distance an object travels in different amounts of time. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the slope of the graph equal to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph has distance in meters on the y-axis and time in seconds on the x-axis. A straight line with a positive slope runs from point (0,0) to point (5,3.5).

choices.content: the average acceleration of the object
the total time the object was traveling
the total distance traveled by the object
the average speed of the object

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph below models the distance an object travels in different amounts of time. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**How is the object's speed related to the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph has distance in meters on the y-axis and time in seconds on the x-axis. A straight line with a positive slope runs from point (0,0) to point (5,3.5).

choices.content: the speed is the largest $x$-axis value plotted
the speed is the largest $y$-axis value plotted
the speed is the slope of the line

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph below is a model of the distance an object travels in different amounts of time. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**If you calculate the slope of the line, you'll find the object's [[☃ dropdown 1]] **
hints: The slope of a line is the change in the $y$-value divided by the change in the $x$-value.

On this graph, the $y$-value is the distance traveled and the $x$-value is the time taken:

$\text{slope}=\dfrac{\text{distance}}{\text{time}}$
Distance divided by time equals average speed. So, if you calculate the slope of the line, you get the object's *speed*.

choices.content: distance traveled
total time traveled
average speed
average acceleration

options.alt: A graph has distance in meters on the y-axis and time in seconds on the x-axis. A straight line with a positive slope runs from point (0,0) to point (5,3.5).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is equal to average speed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\dfrac{\text{distance}}{\text{time}}$
$\dfrac{\text{time}}{\text{distance}}$
$\text{distance}\times\text{time}$
$\text{distance}+\text{time}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

* Average speed equals [[☃ dropdown 2]] divided by [[☃ dropdown 3]].
hints: Average speed is the *rate* of distance traveled.
To calculate a *rate* you divide by time.

choices.content: distance
velocity
acceleration
time
distance
velocity
acceleration
time

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

* To calculate an object's speed, measure the distance traveled and [[☃ dropdown 3]] the time it took to travel that distance.
hints: Average speed is the *rate* of distance traveled.
To calculate a rate you *divide by* time.
Average speed equals distance *divided by* time.

choices.content: add
subtract
multiply by
divide by

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describe acceleration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how quickly an object speeds up
how quickly an object slows down
how quickly an object changes direction
how fast an object moves

assessment_items.question_content:
All of the following statements describe acceleration *except* one. 

**Which statement does NOT describe acceleration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how quickly an object speeds up
how quickly an object slows down
how quickly an object changes direction
how fast an object moves

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following objects are accelerating?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an object that's speeding up
an object that's slowing down
an object that's moving at a constant speed while turning
an object that's moving in a straight line at a fast, constant speed
